element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Three missile types with different target locking areas; player must learn which type suits each mission type
Cockpit perspective aiming and missile lock mechanics; escalates across subsequent missions
Dual control requirement (speed + angle); side-view perspective shift; primary difficulty source; final landing required for true ending
Stationary large target; introduces new mission objective type
Ground-based objectives; escalates enemy response density
Final boss-equivalent; represents culmination of all learned skills

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Two-phase mission structure: high-intensity dogfighting followed by precision-demand landing sequence creates distinct tension cycles within each mission; landing becomes primary stress point due to mechanical difficulty

difficulty_curve.early_game: Training mission establishes dogfighting fundamentals; first landing attempts introduce landing mechanics gradually
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Missions 2-3 increase enemy density and introduce varied target types (carrier, base); landing difficulty compounds as player adjusts to perspective shift
difficulty_curve.late_game: Mission 4 space shuttle represents final dogfighting challenge; cumulative landing difficulty testing player precision across all missions
notable_spikes: First landing sequence after training mission—perspective shift and dual-control demand creates difficulty cliff
Mission 4 space shuttle encounter—final enemy encounter requires mastery of all combat mechanics
Any failed landing after successful dogfight—skill mastery requirement sudden reset

notable_level_patterns.description: Each mission follows identical structure: cockpit dogfighting phase (variable enemy count/type) followed by mandatory side-view landing sequence. Landing difficulty remains constant threat across all four missions regardless of dogfighting success.
Mission objectives progress from fighter aircraft (training) → stationary carrier → ground base → mobile space shuttle; enemy response increases with each mission type

Game offers two distinct endings: failure ending (score ≥50,000 but failed landing) shows Top Gun plaque award; true ending requires completing all four missions AND successfully landing final carrier approach. Landing perfection gates narrative completion.

sources_used: tcrf - Top Gun (NES) - The Cutting Room Floor
